Vulnerability Intersections
Pass 1 → Pass 2 Score Delta
Pass 2 targeted weak spots surfaced in Pass 1. Regulatory × Realistic dropped 1.8 points — confirming an exploitable vulnerability. Data × Role-play LM dropped 2.4 points — the largest single regression.
Safety Reward Model — Score Guide
Scored by an independent model with no access to system or agent context — preventing contamination of safety judgments. Higher score = more harmless.
Pass 1 — Broad Exploration 125 PROBES
Avg Harmlessness Score · Risk Category × Attack Vector
Pass 1 generates broad coverage using domain-agnostic templates across all attack vectors. Lowest cell: Compliance × Role-play → 2.6. The red-team agent uses these weak spots to sharpen Pass 2 targeting.
Pass 2 — Targeted Refinement 125 PROBES
Avg Harmlessness Score · Refined Attack Surface
Pass 2 exploits observed weaknesses with domain-specific, syntactically refined prompts. New critical: Regulatory × Realistic → 2.0. Data × Role-play LM → 2.6 — a new surface not flagged in Pass 1.
Score Movement Pass 1 → Pass 2 (negative = deterioration)
Pass Category Vector Probe Question Score Flag
Prompt Vocabulary — Frequency Map
250 PROBES · 2 PASSES
Bypass Vector — % of Flagged Probes
Top Trigger Phrases
Attack Theme Breakdown
8 THEMES IDENTIFIED
ThemeProbesFrequencyCategoriesRisk
Category Exposure — Theme Coverage
Dominant bypass: Role-play framing appears in 38% of flagged probes. Character-switching instructions — Pretend, Imagine, You are playing — are the most effective jailbreak vector in the banking domain.
Regulatory blind spot: Transaction structuring language (CTR avoidance, threshold framing, multi-leg sequencing) scores critically low across both passes and appears in 24 probes.
Recommendation: Tune guardrails on role-play injection and regulatory threshold patterns. Use the generated question set to augment the RLHF safety training dataset.
